revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:函館港まつり]]
'''Hakodate Minato Matsuri''' (函館港まつり) is a {{ill|summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}} held annually in the city of {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|qid=Q26418|lt=Hakodate|1=Hakodate}}, located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}. The festival first took place from {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|qid=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}} to {{ill|July 3|en|July 3|qid=Q2699|lt=July 3|1=July 3}}, 1935 (Showa 10) to commemorate the 77th anniversary of {{ill|Port of Hakodate|en|Port of Hakodate|qid=Q10896698|lt=Hakodate Port|1=Port of Hakodate}}’s opening and as a recovery celebration for the 1934 {{ill|Great_Fire_of_Hakodate|en|Great_Fire_of_Hakodate|qid=Q10843852|lt=Great Fire of Hakodate|1=Great Fire of Hakodate}}. The event was initiated by then-Mayor {{ill|Moriichi Sakamoto|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriichi Sakamoto|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17991760}}, based on a proposal by shipping union head Tokutaro Tani.<ref>{{Cite web |title=「函館市史」通説編4 7編1章コラム16 |url=http://archives.c.fun.ac.jp/hakodateshishi/tsuusetsu_04/shishi_07-01/shishi_07-01-16.htm |website=archives.c.fun.ac.jp |access-date=2023-11-19}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=函館港まつり(函館港まつり実行委員会)｜東北の観光スポットを探す {{!}} 旅東北 - 東北の観光・旅行情報サイト |url=https://www.tohokukanko.jp/attractions/detail_1001784.html |website=東北の観光・旅行情報サイト「旅東北」 |access-date=2023-11-19 |language=ja |last=一般社団法人東北観光推進機構}}</ref>
== Overview == On March 21, 1934 (Showa 9), the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|en|Great Fire of Hakodate|qid=Q10843852|lt=1934 Hakodate Fire|1=Great Fire of Hakodate}} caused unprecedented damage, with 2,054 deaths and 10,176 buildings destroyed. To commemorate the first anniversary of recovery efforts, the festival was organized under Mayor Sakamoto’s leadership.
Coincidentally, 1935 marked 77 years (in traditional {{ill|East Asian age reckoning|en|East Asian age reckoning|qid=Q850273|lt=Japanese age reckoning|1=East Asian age reckoning}}) since the port’s opening on July 1, 1859 (Ansei 6, June 2). This year was symbolically significant, as 77 is referred to as {{ill|Kiju|ja|喜寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年齢|1=Aged|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q185836}}, celebrating longevity. Despite debates about holding a festival during incomplete recovery, the three-day event attracted an estimated 300,000 attendees, including 70,000 visitors from outside Hakodate.<ref>函館市史 通説編第4巻 pp.677-681</ref>
Initially held in July, Mayor {{ill|Dairiku Mune|ja|宗藤大陸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairiku Mune|1=Tairoku Munetō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q67498374}} proposed moving the event to August, as weather statistics from the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|en|Japan Meteorological Agency|qid=Q860935|lt=Japan Meteorological Agency|1=Japan Meteorological Agency}} indicated lower rainfall probability. August 1 coincided with Hakodate’s municipal anniversary, making the date change practical.<ref>我が街 はこだてタウン誌50th year p.87</ref>
=== Timeline === * 1934 (Showa 9): March 21 - {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|en|Great Fire of Hakodate|qid=Q10843852|lt=1934 Hakodate Fire|1=Great Fire of Hakodate}} * 1935 (Showa 10): ** July 1 - The 77th Anniversary of Hakodate Port Opening Ceremony and the 1st Anniversary of Recovery from the Great Fire (1st Minato Matsuri).
* 1936 (Showa 11): July 1 - 2nd festival held. * 1941 (Showa 16): Festival scaled down due to war. * 1943 (Showa 18): Cancelled due to wartime restrictions. * 1946 (Showa 21): Resumed after the war. * 1960 (Showa 35): The first {{ill|10,000 People Parade|ja|一万人踊りパレード|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10,000 People Parade|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1981 (Showa 56): The creation of the {{ill|Squid Dance|ja|いか踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Squid Dance|qq=}}. * 1993 (Heisei 5): Adjustments made after the {{ill|1993 Okushiri earthquake|en|1993 Okushiri earthquake|qid=Q4588561|lt=Hokkaido Nansei-Oki Earthquake|1=1993 Okushiri earthquake}}. * 2021 (Reiwa 3): Cancelled due to COVID-19. * 2023 (Reiwa 5): Full-scale resumption.
== Events == ; Hakodate Port Festival Fireworks : Hosted by the Hokkaido Shimbun Hakodate Branch, fireworks are launched from {{ill|Midori-no-Shima|ja|緑の島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-no-Shima|1=Midorinoshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11607759}} in {{ill|Hakodate Bay|ja|函館湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bay|1=Hakodate Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11396019}}.<ref>[https://www.hakobura.jp/spots/685 "ふれあいイカ広場"] Hakodate Official Tourism Site, Hakobura, accessed April 10, 2024.</ref> ; Wasshoi Hakodate (Parade) : Initially held in 1960, this parade allows citizens to dance through the main streets. Participants include groups performing traditional dances, creative styles, and the {{ill|Squid Dance|ja|いか踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Squid Dance|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史 通説編第4巻 pP677-p681</ref> ; Hakodate Station Night Market : About 80 stalls set up along Hakodate streets, offering food and goods.<ref>[https://www3.nhk.or.jp/sapporo-news/20230801/7000059650.html "函館港まつり始まる"] NHK News, updated August 1, 2023.</ref>
== Music == ; Hakodate Port Dance : Written by {{ill|Mikihiko Nagata|en|Mikihiko Nagata|qid=Q3335075|lt=Mikihiko Nagata|1=Mikihiko Nagata}} and composed by {{ill|Shinpei Nakayama|en|Shinpei Nakayama|qid=Q764161|lt=Shinpei Nakayama|1=Shinpei Nakayama}} in 1935.<ref>函館市史 通説編第3巻 p865－p866</ref>
== Port Opening Day == The port opening anniversary was originally observed as a public holiday, including closures of government offices and schools. However, the holiday designation was abolished in 1986.<ref>"[https://www.city.hakodate.hokkaido.jp/citizensvoice/docs/2018082800010/ 開港記念日]" Hakodate City, Citizens' Voice, 2018.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Great_Fire_of_Hakodate|en|Great_Fire_of_Hakodate|qid=Q10843852|lt=Great Fire of Hakodate|1=Great Fire of Hakodate}} * {{ill|Squid Dance|ja|いか踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Squid Dance|qq=}} * {{ill|Yokohama Port Festival|ja|横浜開港祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Port Festival|1=Yokohama Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11543279}}
== External Links == * [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Minato Matsuri Official Website] ''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} – Features dances like the {{ill|Squid Dance|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Squid Dance|qq=}}. * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Aomori Nebuta Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Nebuta Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Tohoku|lt=Three Great Festivals of Tohoku|lt=Three Great Festivals of Tohoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Tohoku|qq=}}. ''' {{ill|Hirosaki Neputa Matsuri|lt=Hirosaki Neputa Matsuri|lt=Hirosaki Neputa Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Neputa Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} ''' {{ill|Sendai Tanabata Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Tanabata Festival|qq=}} – One of the Three Great Festivals of Tohoku. * {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Akita Kantō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanto Festival|qq=}} – One of the Three Great Festivals of Tohoku. * {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Fukushima Waraji Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Waraji Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Yagibushi Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Fukagawa Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukagawa Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Edo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Edo|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gujo Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gujo Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Matsuri|qq=}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura Onsen Fire Festival|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8061163}} * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Awa Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Odori|qq=}} – Its {{ill|Tokushima City Awa Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Awa Odori|qq=}} is one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} – One of the Three Great Festivals of Shikoku. * {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Lantern Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sabantuy|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabantuy|qq=}} – A summer festival held along the middlereaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volga River|qq=}} in {{ill|Russia|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russia|qq=}}. * {{ill|Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}
